data.title: feasibility review misses infrastructure protocol constraints (HTTPS required for Cognito callback URLs)
data.description:
## Incident Overview
A host project configuring Cognito for a staging environment failed because the environment only supported HTTP, but Cognito requires HTTPS for callback URLs. The feasibility review (dso:feasibility-reviewer) did not surface this constraint during planning.

## Root Cause
The feasibility reviewer does not include infrastructure protocol/transport-layer constraints in its check scope. AWS Cognito (and similar OAuth/OIDC providers) mandate HTTPS for redirect/callback URIs as a security requirement. This is a known, documented constraint that should be flagged during feasibility review when an epic involves OAuth/OIDC authentication setup.

## Impact
Epic failed mid-implementation when the callback URL registration was rejected, requiring rework of the environment setup and delaying delivery.

## Expected Fix
The feasibility reviewer's prompt/checklist should include a check for OAuth/OIDC-specific infrastructure requirements — specifically that HTTPS-capable environments are available when the epic involves user authentication via external identity providers (Cognito, Auth0, Okta, etc.). The check should fire when the epic description or story titles reference OAuth, OIDC, SSO, Cognito, or similar authentication flows.
